meta.title: EB5 Pathway | U.S. Green Card Through Investment (EB-5 Visa)
meta.description: Learn how the EB-5 Immigrant Investor Program can get you and your family a U.S. Green Card. Free consultation with a licensed EB-5 advisor at InvestAmerica.

hero.title: A U.S. Green Card for you and your family, through a secure investment
hero.subtitle: We connect qualified investors with InvestAmerica, a licensed EB-5 broker-dealer with a track record of due diligence and a strong project approval history reported by its clients. Get honest, no-obligation guidance on whether EB-5 is right for you.
hero.stat2Label: U.S. jobs your investment must create
hero.stat3Label: Application that can cover your whole family
hero.disclaimer: EB-5 is a U.S. immigration investment program. All investments carry risk, including possible loss of principal and immigration denial. This site provides educational information and refers interested investors to a licensed broker-dealer; it does not provide legal or investment advice.

benefits.title: Why families choose the EB-5 path
benefits.subtitle: A U.S. Green Card opens doors that go far beyond the investment itself.

items.title: Permanent residency for your whole family
items.description: A single EB-5 investment can cover you, your spouse, and unmarried children under 21 — all obtaining lawful permanent residence together.

items.title: Live, work and study anywhere in the U.S.
items.description: No employer sponsorship, no job offer required. Green Card holders can work for any employer, start a business, or be self-employed anywhere in the country.

items.title: World-class education for your children
items.description: Green Card holders qualify for in-state tuition and have access to U.S. public schools and universities on the same terms as citizens.

items.title: A path to U.S. citizenship
items.description: After holding a Green Card for five years, investors and their families may become eligible to apply for U.S. citizenship.

investment.title: A regulated, security-focused investment
investment.subtitle: EB-5 is a real capital investment in U.S. job-creating projects — not a donation or a fee. It is reviewed under U.S. securities and immigration law.
investment.teaLabel: Targeted Employment Area (rural or high-unemployment area)
investment.jobsNote: Every EB-5 investment must create at least 10 full-time jobs for U.S. workers within the required timeframe — this is verified by USCIS before a Green Card is approved.

items.title: Licensed broker-dealer oversight
items.description: InvestAmerica offers securities through Sequence Financial Specialists LLC, a FINRA/SIPC member. Every recommendation goes through a registered representative bound by securities regulations.

items.title: Rigorous project due diligence
items.description: Each investment opportunity is screened across more than 40 factors — financial projections, sponsor track record, background checks, and immigration risk — before it is offered to investors.

items.title: Objective, independent advice
items.description: Advisors are compensated to represent your interests, not to push a single project. You review multiple vetted opportunities before deciding.

process.title: How the process works
process.subtitle: From your first conversation to filing, here's what the journey typically looks like.

steps.title: Free introductory call
steps.description: Tell us about your goals and background. We answer your questions and explain honestly whether EB-5 fits your situation.

steps.title: Review investment options
steps.description: InvestAmerica presents vetted, due-diligenced projects that match your investment amount, industry preference, and timeline.

steps.title: Source of funds & subscription
steps.description: With guidance from immigration and securities professionals, you document your source of funds and complete subscription paperwork.

steps.title: File your I-526E petition
steps.description: Your immigration attorney files the petition with USCIS, backed by the project's business plan and job-creation evidence.

steps.title: Conditional and then permanent residency
steps.description: Once approved, you and your family obtain conditional Green Cards, followed by permanent residency after the investment's job-creation requirements are confirmed.

trust.title: Why go through InvestAmerica
trust.subtitle: A specialized, securities-regulated platform built specifically for EB-5 investors.
points: Team of licensed registered representatives with deep EB-5 and private placement experience
Diverse platform of direct investment opportunities across real estate, hospitality and infrastructure
Consistent performance through a historically volatile EB-5 regulatory landscape
Advice aligned with your best interests, not driven by any single project's sales targets

faq.title: Frequently asked questions
faq.subtitle: Can't find your answer? Ask our chat assistant in the corner, or talk to an advisor directly.

items.q: What is the EB-5 Immigrant Investor Program?
items.a: EB-5 is a U.S. immigration category created by Congress that grants permanent residency (a Green Card) to foreign investors who make a qualifying investment in a U.S. business that creates at least 10 full-time jobs for U.S. workers.

items.q: How much do I need to invest?
items.a: The minimum is $800,000 if the project is located in a Targeted Employment Area (a rural or high-unemployment area), or $1,050,000 for projects outside a TEA. Most EB-5 regional center projects are structured to qualify at the $800,000 level.

items.q: Does the investment cover my spouse and children?
items.a: Yes. A single qualifying investment and petition can include your spouse and any unmarried children under 21 at the time of filing.

No. Like any securities investment, EB-5 capital is at risk, including possible loss of principal, and there is no guarantee of investment return or immigration approval. That is exactly why due diligence and working with a licensed broker-dealer matters.

items.q: How long does the process take?
items.a: Timelines vary with USCIS processing volumes and your country of birth, but investors typically see conditional permanent residency within roughly 2 to 4 years of filing, with some cases faster or slower depending on visa category backlogs.

items.q: Do I need to live in the U.S. to invest?
items.a: No. You can begin the EB-5 process, including making the investment and filing your petition, while living outside the United States. Physical presence generally becomes relevant once you enter on your immigrant visa or adjust status.

items.q: What is a Targeted Employment Area (TEA)?
items.a: A TEA is a rural area or an area with unemployment at least 150% of the national average. Projects located in a TEA qualify for the lower $800,000 investment threshold.

items.q: What does InvestAmerica actually do for me?
items.a: InvestAmerica is a licensed broker-dealer platform (through Sequence Financial Specialists LLC, FINRA/SIPC member) that vets EB-5 projects, provides objective investment guidance, and works alongside your immigration attorney throughout the process. This site refers interested investors to their team for a free, no-obligation consultation.

leadForm.title: Request your free consultation
leadForm.subtitle: Share a few details and a licensed EB-5 advisor from InvestAmerica will reach out — no cost, no obligation.
leadForm.name: Full name
leadForm.message: Anything else you'd like us to know? (optional)
leadForm.consent: I agree to be contacted by InvestAmerica and its referral partner about EB-5 investment opportunities. I understand this is not legal or investment advice.
leadForm.success: Thank you! Your request has been sent. An advisor will contact you within 1–2 business days.
leadForm.error: Something went wrong sending your request. Please try again or email us directly.

footer.about: EB5 Pathway is an independent informational site that helps prospective investors understand the EB-5 Immigrant Investor Program and connects them with InvestAmerica, a licensed EB-5 broker-dealer platform, for a free consultation.
footer.disclaimer: This website does not provide legal, tax or investment advice and is not a broker-dealer or investment advisor. Securities, when offered, are offered through Sequence Financial Specialists LLC, member FINRA/SIPC, dba InvestAmerica. All investments involve risk, including possible loss of principal. Immigration outcomes are determined solely by U.S. Citizenship and Immigration Services (USCIS) and are not guaranteed.
footer.brokerCheck: Check the broker-dealer's record on FINRA BrokerCheck
